texto: In a swamp, as in meditation, you begin to glimpse how elusive, how inherently insubstantial, how fleeting our thoughts are, our identities. There is magic in this moist world, in how the mind lets go, slips into sleepy water, circles and nuzzles the banks of palmetto and wild iris, how it seeps across dreams, smears them into the upright world, rots the wood of treasure chests, welcomes the body home.
explicacao: Citação de Barbara Hurd sobre temas ligados a motivacao. “In a swamp, as in meditation, you begin to glimpse how elusive, how inherently insubstantial, how…” articula motivacao, body, circle em registro de motivacao, com relevância para contextos como circle e dream.

frase_original: In a swamp, as in meditation, you begin to glimpse how elusive, how inherently insubstantial, how fleeting our thoughts are, our identities. There is magic in this moist world, in how the mind lets go, slips into sleepy water, circles and nuzzles the banks of palmetto and wild iris, how it seeps across dreams, smears them into the upright world, rots the wood of treasure chests, welcomes the body home.

texto: To love a swamp, however, is to love what is muted and marginal, what exists in the shadows, what shoulders its way out of mud and scurries along the damp edges of what is most commonly praised. And sometimes its invisibility is a blessing. Swamps and bogs are places of transition and wild growth, breeding grounds, experimental labs where organisms and ideas have the luxury of being out of the spotlight, where the imagination can mutate and mate, send tendrils into and out of the water.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Barbara Hurd sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. A afirmação “To love a swamp, however, is to love what is muted and marginal, what exists in the shadows, what…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, bog, change, útil em momentos de mudanca e termino.
